subject: fix: close 4 critical durable-runtime safety gaps (#63)

decision: Adopt A2A-aligned orchestrator/worker nomenclature; drop invented 'mayor protocol' term
why: Web research: 'mayor protocol' is not an industry term. Standard is hierarchical/supervisor-worker orchestration; A2A (Agent2Agent, Linux Foundation, has a Go SDK) models tasks with a lifecycle+id+state which maps onto plandag. MCP=tools/context, A2A=agent-to-agent. radioactive-ralph's planning variant is an 'orchestrator agent' coordinating 'worker variants'.

decision: Provider support: keep claude+codex+opencode, REMOVE gemini, ADD cursor-agent, evaluate Antigravity CLI
why: Gemini CLI deprecated 2026-06-18 (auth endpoint 410 Gone; every gemini command fails), replaced by Antigravity CLI (Go, closed-source). cursor-agent is actively maintained (not the 'cursor' editor binary). opencode exposes 'run --format json', 'stats' (spend), ACP+MCP — clean declarative-provider fit; locally bound to GLM 5.2. Detection must distinguish cursor (editor) from cursor-agent (agent CLI).

decision: DROP cursor-agent too; local-only provider set is claude + codex + opencode
why: User: cursor-agent delegates the agent loop to a remote runner (Cursor cloud-agent handoff), so it fails the local-only focus. Rule: a provider must run the agentic loop + tool execution + repo access LOCALLY (calling a hosted model API for inference is fine — claude/codex/opencode all do). cursor-agent's remote delegation disqualifies it. Detection heuristics still recognize/label deprecated (gemini) and remote-delegating (cursor-agent) CLIs to explain WHY they aren't offered.

decision: Define 'local-only' precisely: no delegation to a cloud SURFACE (agent/session control surface must be local). Hosted model inference is allowed.
why: User clarification. The disqualifier is a CLI that hands the agent SESSION/CONTROL surface to a cloud service (cursor-agent cloud-agent handoff; web dashboards; remote session runners you attach to). Calling a hosted model API for inference is NOT disqualifying — every supported CLI does that. Criterion: the CLI must own the agent loop + tool execution + session control on the local machine with no cloud-hosted control surface in the loop. opencode qualifies via its local 'opencode run' path; we bind ONLY that path and never opencode serve/web/--share/attach (those introduce a cloud/remote surface).

decision: Antigravity CLI (agy 1.1.3) is a local-provider CANDIDATE to spike in U3, not dropped
why: agy is installed and responds locally to models/agents. Surface is claude/codex-shaped: --print (non-interactive single prompt), --model, --mode (accept-edits/plan), --continue/--conversation resume, --add-dir workspace, --sandbox. Owns the agent loop locally. Open question: --print errored on a bare probe (likely auth/project setup), and --project/--new-project hint at a project abstraction that must be confirmed NOT to be a cloud control surface. Spike in U3: if --print is confirmed local-surface, add agy as the 4th local provider (the Google-lineage slot vacated by dead gemini). Models are Gemini 3.5 Flash / 3.1 Pro.

decision: Reframe U4: durable self-correcting planning is the Fixit<->Professor pairing, not a new standalone orchestrator variant
why: User: professor and fixit should be two halves of a coin enabling durable, self-corrected planning of any problem. Fixit = advisor/decomposer (scans, ROI-scores, writes plan DAG, recommends peers). Professor = plan->execute->reflect executor. Together they form the closed durable loop: Fixit decomposes+recommends -> Professor executes with reflection -> reflection feeds back to Fixit to re-plan/self-correct. The A2A orchestrator/worker pattern is realized by this pairing over the existing plandag task lifecycle, rather than inventing a separate orchestrator profile. U4 becomes: evolve professor+fixit into this coordinated loop.

subject: chore(agent-state): bootstrap the build-out directive + decisions log
decision: adopt A2A orchestrator/worker nomenclature and the local-only
why: gemini CLI is dead (410 Gone since 2026-06-18); cursor-agent delegates
